title: LoL Esports |
content:
The countdown is on. The 2026 LCS Spring Finals Driven by Kia is almost here, and the best teams in North America are preparing to compete in Tempe, Arizona. Final qualification for Spring Finals takes place June 6-7, where the final three teams will secure their spot on stage at Arizona State University. Cloud9 Kia has already punched their ticket through an impressive playoff run, leaving two remaining spots up for grabs.

With the LCS bringing live events to fans across North America more consistently than ever before, Spring Finals marks the beginning of a new era for in-person League of Legends esports.

There’s still time to secure your seats for Finals Weekend. Fans can choose from:

In addition to the action on stage, fans attending Spring Finals can look forward to opportunities to participate in community activations, explore partner experiences, purchase LCS merch, and celebrate alongside the LCS community throughout the weekend.

Kia Booth:At LCS Spring Finals Driven by Kia, fans can visit the Kia booth to participate in Kia Rift Trivia, an interactive experience that tests their League of Legends knowledge for a chance to win in-game prizes. The Kia Rift Trivia will showcase the first-ever 2027 Kia Telluride Turbo-Hybrid, offering fans an up-close look at Kia’s latest hybrid SUV. The first 500 fans to enter Mullett Arena each day will receive a complimentary water bottle courtesy of Kia!

Cosplayer Photo Ops:Be on the lookout for the Vayne & the new champion Locke roaming the Mullett Arena or in the photo op space near the Kia Booth!

title: Why 2026 is the perfect time to get into League of Legends esports
summary: Why 2026 is the perfect time to get into League of Legends esports  Red Bull
content:
The competitive League of Legends ecosystem is buzzing following the latest announcement regarding 'Why 2026 is the perfect time to get into League of Legends esports'. As the season progresses, this development has quickly taken center stage, drawing intense scrutiny from fans, analysts, and professional players alike. The core update highlights a significant shift that is expected to have wide-ranging consequences for the regional leagues and international tournaments.

Riot Games continues to refine the competitive structure, balancing the game's high skill ceiling with spectator entertainment. The professional scene is an ever-evolving battleground where even the smallest adjustment can make or break a team's championship aspirations. Every coach, analyst, and player must stay ahead of the curve to remain competitive.

Speaking on the recent events, seasoned esports analysts noted that 'Why 2026 is the perfect time to get into League of Legends esports  Red Bull' is not just a temporary trend but a fundamental shift in how teams will approach their upcoming matchdays. 'Draft priority and map control are going to be completely redefined over the next few weeks,' remarked a prominent league caster. 'Teams that fail to recognize the gravity of this change will find themselves falling behind very quickly in the standings.'

As the community awaits the upcoming weekend matches, anticipation continues to build. Fans can catch all the high-stakes action live on official streaming channels, complete with expert desk analysis and live regional broadcasts. With so much on the line, the next chapter of the 2026 competitive season promises to deliver some of the most memorable moments in League of Legends esports history.

title: Riot Games introduces First Selection rule for League of Legends esports in 2026
summary: Riot Games introduces First Selection rule for League of Legends esports in 2026  Esports Charts
content:
The new League of Legends season has not yet started, but things have been moving pretty quickly in the off-season. On January 8, Riot Games made another announcement that is bound to affect the upcoming esports season.

The American organizer introduced the "First Selection" feature to LoL esports for the 2026 season.This new rule willserve asa new approach to showcase thenewPick & Ban phase during matches.

Under the new First Selection approach, aimed at refining how the massively popular MOBAis playedat the highest level by balancing the variance in side win rates. This fresh rule will see teams who previously earned side selection now also getting first selection, meaning they can choose one of the following:

Once a side makes that decision, the opponent willget itschoice of the remainingoption.So if Team A chooses Blue Side, Team B willchoosewhethertheydraft first or second.If Team A chooses First Pick, Team B willchoosewhich side they'll play on.

Riot Games added in its announcement that this update will attempt to give professional teams more strategic flexibility to shape their game plan based on comfort, preparation, and opponent scouting, not on a baked-in advantage tied to color.

First Selection will roll out in all regions at the start of the season, with the first regional leagues to kick off being LCK and LPL, on January 14. The other four circuits will follow suit, with the last event to kick off being the LCS on January 24.

title: League of Legends 2026 Tournament Calendar: Worlds, MSI, Regional Leagues & Tier-2 Events
summary: League of Legends 2026 Tournament Calendar: Worlds, MSI, Regional Leagues & Tier-2 Events  Esports Charts
content:
The 2026League of Legendsesports season kicks off today, once again launching one of the longest and most layered competitive calendars in all of esports. Unlike scenes built around a handful of standalone events, League of Legends operates on a year-long ecosystem where dozens of leagues and tournaments run in parallel, intersect and feed into one another.

That structure is also what makes the scene confusing from the outside. Competition spans multiple tiers, from global events likeWorldsandMid-Season Invitational(MSI) to top-level regional leagues and further down to national and sub-regional circuits that quietly develop future talent. Depending on who you ask, “the season” might mean only Worlds, a full year of Tier-1 regional play, or even following specific Tier-2 leagues week by week.

That’s exactly why this calendar exists. Instead of listing tournaments in a simple chronological order, we’ve structured the 2026 League of Legends season by competitive level. This way, readers can focus on what actually matters to them, whether that’s international championships or major regional leagues.

Photo courtesy of Christina Oh/Riot Games

The Americas Cup is a newly introduced international event launched following the reorganization of the LTA ecosystem. It opens the international calendar for the 2026 season, bringing together the strongest teams from the winter portion of the season across North America and Brazil.

Beyond regional bragging rights, the Americas Cup carries direct competitive stakes. The event determines which additional Americas team qualifies for First Stand, making it an early-season gateway into the international calendar and an important link between regional play and global competition.

title: Season Start 2026: LoL Esports
content:
The 2026 League of Legends Esports season has arrived, and the excitement is already building. After an unforgettable 2025 marked by dramatic storylines, major format shifts, and T1 cementing their legacy with a historic third consecutive World Championship, the stage is set for an even bigger year. With fresh updates shaping the meta and a full calendar of high-stakes competition, here’s everything to look forward to as the 2026 season gets underway.

Make sure to tune in to your favorite region as competition kicks off beginning this month!

There’s no better way to kick off the year than with the first global tournament of the season: First Stand.

From March 16–22, FST heads to São Paulo, Brazil, live from the Riot Games Arena, the beating heart of CBLOL. Brazil has long been home to some of the world’s most passionate fans, and we couldn't be more excited to bring an international event back since 2017.

The 2026 edition brings together eight teams, representing the global strength of League of Legends Esports:

Every match will be a best-of-five, ensuring deeper adaptation, more high-stakes moments, and an early-season showcase of regional identity and individual brilliance. With Fearless Draft shaping the future of how teams adapt across a series, expect creativity, tension, and more opportunities for emerging strategies to take center stage. Every match matters, as FST placement will have a direct impact on MSI seeding, including a Play-In Stage bye for the winning region.
